title: SQL Window Functions
description: Window functions, partitioning, ranking, lag/lead, running totals, and frame specifications.

questions.text: What does the OVER() clause do in SQL?

options.text: Defines the table to scan
Defines the window (set of rows) over which the window function operates
Overrides the WHERE clause filter
Applies an aggregate function to the entire result set only

questions.text: What does PARTITION BY do inside an OVER() clause?

options.text: Sorts the rows within the window
Divides the result set into partitions (groups) so the window function is applied independently to each partition
Limits the number of rows in the result
Joins rows from multiple tables

questions.text: What is the key difference between window functions and GROUP BY?

options.text: Window functions collapse rows into groups; GROUP BY does not
Window functions compute values across related rows while KEEPING all individual rows; GROUP BY reduces rows to one per group
GROUP BY can use OVER(); window functions cannot
Window functions are always faster than GROUP BY

questions.text: What does ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY Salary DESC) assign?

options.text: A rank with gaps for tied values
A unique sequential integer to each row in the specified order, with no gaps or ties
The same number to rows with equal salary
A random number

questions.text: Employees have salaries: 5000, 5000, 3000. What does RANK() OVER (ORDER BY Salary DESC) assign?
What does DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY Salary DESC) assign for salaries 5000, 5000, 3000?
What is the difference between RANK() and DENSE_RANK()?

options.text: RANK() assigns unique integers; DENSE_RANK() allows ties
RANK() skips numbers after a tie; DENSE_RANK() does not skip numbers
DENSE_RANK() skips numbers; RANK() does not
They are identical

questions.text: What does NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY Salary) do?

options.text: Returns the 4th highest salary
Divides the result set into 4 equal buckets (quartiles) and assigns each row a bucket number
Returns the top 4 rows
Assigns a tile number from 1 to 4 randomly

questions.text: What does LAG(Salary, 1) OVER (ORDER BY HireDate) return?

options.text: The next row's salary in the ordered window
The previous row's salary (1 row behind) in the ordered window
The salary lagged by 1 second in time
A cumulative lag calculation

questions.text: What does LEAD(Salary, 1) OVER (ORDER BY HireDate) return?

options.text: The previous row's salary
The next row's salary (1 row ahead) in the ordered window
The leading (maximum) salary
NULL always

questions.text: What does FIRST_VALUE(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY DeptID ORDER BY Salary DESC) return?

options.text: The minimum salary in the department
The salary of the first row in the ordered partition — the highest salary in each department
The first salary value inserted into the table
Always NULL

questions.text: What does LAST_VALUE() return and why might it behave unexpectedly without a frame specification?

options.text: It always returns the last row of the entire table
Without a ROWS/RANGE frame, the default frame ends at the current row, so LAST_VALUE returns the current row's value, not the partition's last row
It returns NULL for all rows except the last
It only works with PARTITION BY

questions.text: Which query computes a running total of sales ordered by SaleDate?

options.text: SELECT SaleDate, Amount, SUM(Amount) OVER (ORDER BY SaleDate) AS RunningTotal FROM Sales
SELECT SaleDate, Amount, RUNNING_SUM(Amount) FROM Sales ORDER BY SaleDate
SELECT SaleDate, SUM(Amount) FROM Sales GROUP BY SaleDate
SELECT SaleDate, CUMSUM(Amount) OVER SaleDate FROM Sales

questions.text: What does AVG(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY DeptID) compute?

options.text: The overall average salary across the entire table
The average salary within each department, displayed on every row (without reducing rows)
The cumulative average salary
The average salary for each row individually

questions.text: What does the frame specification ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW define?

options.text: A window covering all rows after the current row
A window from the first row of the partition to the current row — used for running totals
A window covering only the current row
A window covering a fixed number of rows before the current row

questions.text: What is the difference between ROWS and RANGE frame specifications?

options.text: ROWS operates on a physical number of rows; RANGE operates on logical value ranges (all rows with the same ORDER BY value are included in the same peer group)
RANGE operates on physical rows; ROWS on logical groups
They are identical
ROWS allows UNBOUNDED; RANGE does not

questions.text: Can you filter results based on a window function result directly in the WHERE clause?

options.text: Yes, you can use WHERE ROW_NUMBER() OVER(...) = 1 directly
No — window functions cannot be in WHERE; wrap the query in a subquery or CTE and filter there
Yes, but only with RANK() and DENSE_RANK()
Yes, in the HAVING clause only

questions.text: How do you use ROW_NUMBER() to return only the top 1 salary per department?

options.text: SELECT * FROM Employees WHERE ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY DeptID ORDER BY Salary DESC) = 1
WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY DeptID ORDER BY Salary DESC) AS rn FROM Employees) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1
SELECT TOP 1 * FROM Employees GROUP BY DeptID ORDER BY Salary DESC
SELECT MAX(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY DeptID) FROM Employees

questions.text: What does LAG(Salary, 1, 0) OVER (ORDER BY HireDate) return for the first row where there is no previous row?

options.text: NULL
An error
0 — the third argument is the default value when there is no previous row
The first row's salary

questions.text: What does ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW define in a window frame?

options.text: A window covering the current row and all rows after it
A sliding window of 3 rows — the 2 rows before the current row plus the current row
A window covering 2 rows after the current row
The current row only

questions.text: What is a practical use case for NTILE()?

options.text: Generating sequential row numbers
Dividing data into quartiles, deciles, or percentiles for reporting and analysis
Finding the previous row's value
Computing cumulative sums

questions.text: Can a query contain multiple different window functions each with its own OVER() clause?

options.text: No, only one OVER() clause is allowed per query
Yes — multiple window functions with different OVER() definitions can exist in the same SELECT
Yes, but all must share the same PARTITION BY
Only if they use the same ORDER BY

questions.text: What does ROWS BETWEEN CURRENT ROW AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING define?

options.text: A window from the start of the partition to the current row
A window from the current row to the last row of the partition
A window covering only the current row
An invalid frame specification

questions.text: Which window function would you use to implement pagination (e.g., page 2 of sorted results)?

options.text: RANK()
NTILE()
ROW_NUMBER()
DENSE_RANK()

questions.text: What does SUM(Amount) OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY OrderDate ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) compute?

options.text: Total amount for the entire table
A running total of Amount for each customer, ordered by date
Total amount per customer in a single value
Average amount per customer

questions.text: What does PERCENT_RANK() compute?

options.text: The number of rows as a percentage of the total
The relative rank of a row as a value between 0 and 1, calculated as (rank - 1) / (total rows - 1)
The row's value as a percentage of the partition sum
The NTILE bucket number as a percentage

questions.text: In what order does SQL Server process window functions relative to WHERE, GROUP BY, and HAVING?

options.text: Window functions are evaluated before WHERE
Window functions are evaluated after FROM, WHERE, GROUP BY, and HAVING — they see the final filtered/grouped result set
Window functions are evaluated before GROUP BY
Window functions are evaluated at the same time as WHERE

questions.text: What does CUME_DIST() compute?

options.text: A cumulative distribution value between 0 and 1, representing the fraction of partition rows with values less than or equal to the current row
The distance between the current row and the last row
A cumulative sum divided by the total count
The cumulative distinct count

questions.text: Which is the correct way to compute a moving average of the last 3 rows including the current row?

options.text: AVG(Amount) OVER (ORDER BY SaleDate ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
AVG(Amount) OVER (ORDER BY SaleDate RANGE BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW)
MOVING_AVG(Amount, 3) OVER (ORDER BY SaleDate)
AVG(Amount) OVER (ORDER BY SaleDate LIMIT 3)

questions.text: When should you use DENSE_RANK() instead of RANK()?

options.text: When you want gaps in ranking after ties
When you need consecutive rank numbers with no gaps after tied values
When the data has no ties
When you need unique row numbers regardless of ties
